motion: Governments should adopt compulsory voting in national elections.
Term limits should be imposed on members of Congress.
The Electoral College should be abolished in the United States.
Campaign donations from corporations should be banned.
Political debates should require real-time fact-checking on screen.
Independent redistricting commissions should replace partisan map drawing.
Voting should be mandatory for citizens under 70 unless they opt out with cause.
Social media companies should be treated as political publishers during election season.
National referendums should be binding rather than advisory.
Public campaign financing should replace most private fundraising.
Lowering the voting age to 16 would strengthen democracy.
Legislators should be prohibited from trading individual stocks while in office.
Coalition governments produce better outcomes than two-party dominance.
National service should be encouraged through tax incentives rather than mandates.
Populism is a necessary corrective to elite-driven politics.
Debates between political candidates should include citizen juries asking questions.
Parliamentary systems are superior to presidential systems.
States should rank-choice vote all federal elections.
The filibuster does more harm than good.
Local governments should have greater autonomy from federal control.
Political parties should be required to hold open primaries.
Direct democracy tools are overused in modern politics.
National IDs would improve democratic participation.
Constitutional amendments should require simpler ratification thresholds.
Cabinet positions should require relevant professional experience.
Elected officials should face maximum age limits for holding office.
National elections should be public holidays.
Political advertisements should be banned in the final week before an election.
Constitutions should explicitly protect digital rights.
Coalition-building is a better sign of leadership than ideological purity.
Mayors should have more influence over regional economic policy.
Foreign lobbying should be prohibited entirely.
Supreme court justices should serve fixed terms.
Emergency executive powers are too broad in most democracies.
Presidential debates should include fact-check penalties for repeat falsehoods.
Democracies should ban parties that openly reject democratic norms.
Local elections should be nonpartisan by default.
Public opinion polls do more harm than good during campaigns.
Cabinets should reflect ideological diversity rather than strict party loyalty.
Political dynasties weaken democracy.
Protest movements are more effective than party politics at driving change.
Artificial general intelligence research should be globally regulated.
Gene editing in human embryos should be permitted for disease prevention.
Private space exploration benefits humanity more than it harms it.
Social media algorithms should be open to public audit.
The benefits of facial recognition technology outweigh the risks.
Autonomous vehicles should be allowed on public roads before they are perfect.
Nuclear energy should be central to climate strategy.
Scientific journals should make all publicly funded research free to access.
Children under 16 should be banned from using generative AI companions.
Space colonization should be prioritized over deep-ocean exploration.
Tech companies should be legally liable for foreseeable harms caused by their AI tools.
Human enhancement technology should be embraced rather than feared.
There should be a global moratorium on lethal autonomous weapons.
Open-source AI models create more good than harm.
Governments should fund moon and Mars missions even during economic downturns.
Consumer DNA databases should be tightly restricted by law.
Online anonymity does more harm than good.
Data privacy should take priority over personalized convenience.
Quantum computing research deserves massive public investment.
School systems should teach AI literacy as early as middle school.
Biohacking should be regulated like medicine.
The precautionary principle slows scientific progress too much.
Internet access should be treated as a public utility.
The right to repair should override most manufacturer restrictions.
Brain-computer interfaces should be legal for non-medical use.
Researchers should be allowed to clone extinct species where feasible.
Robots should be granted limited legal personhood if they become conscious.
Governments should ban deepfake political ads.
Medical AI should be allowed to overrule physicians in some narrow cases.
Crypto should be regulated like securities by default.
Digital platforms should compensate users for monetizing their data.
Humanity should build a planetary defense system against asteroids.
The metaverse was a distraction rather than a genuine technological frontier.
Lab-grown meat should receive major public subsidies.
Smartphones should be banned in elementary schools.
AI-generated research papers should be prohibited from academic publication.
Technological unemployment is a bigger threat than inflation.
Internet platforms should verify the identity of all users behind the scenes.
Consumer tech design intentionally exploits human weakness.
Governments should require interoperability between major messaging platforms.
The race to build smarter AI is structurally unsafe.
Free will is an illusion.
A meaningful life matters more than a happy life.
Utilitarianism is the best basis for public policy.
Moral relativism is more dangerous than moral absolutism.
People have a duty to seek truth even when it is uncomfortable.
The pursuit of authenticity is overrated.
Personal identity persists even if memory does not.
Ends can justify means in extreme cases.
Suffering is more morally urgent than happiness.
Stoicism is the best philosophy for modern life.
Humans are naturally cooperative rather than selfish.
Revenge can be morally justified.
There is no ethical consumption under capitalism.
Moral progress is real.
Individual freedom should outweigh social harmony.
Death gives life meaning.
Consciousness cannot be fully explained by physical processes alone.
The self is a social construct.
Justice matters more than mercy.
Radical honesty is ethically preferable to tact.
Nihilism is a rational response to modern life.
The search for objective morality is misguided.
Virtue ethics is more practical than deontology.
A society should prioritize dignity over equality.
Humans are bad by nature.
People owe more moral duty to strangers than they usually admit.
Ignorance can sometimes be morally innocent.
Justice requires unequal treatment in unequal circumstances.
Forgiveness is morally superior to revenge.
Pleasure is not the highest human good.
A lie that prevents harm can be virtuous.
There is a moral duty to improve oneself.
Humility is more important than confidence.
Most people value certainty more than truth.
Meaning can exist without any grand purpose.
Moral luck makes true justice impossible.
Love is partly a choice rather than merely a feeling.
People should be judged more by intention than outcome.
Civilization depends more on restraint than creativity.
Pessimism is underrated as a worldview.
Hope is a rational virtue.
Religion remains essential for moral formation.
Secular societies are better equipped to protect freedom of belief.
Faith-based schools should receive public funding.
Religious exemptions from general laws should be narrowly limited.
Science and religion are fundamentally compatible.
Public officials should keep personal religious reasoning out of policy decisions.
Religious traditions should evolve rapidly to reflect modern values.
Atheism provides a stronger foundation for ethics than organized religion.
Interfaith dialogue does more to reduce conflict than secular activism.
Missionary work is ethically defensible.
Places of worship should pay taxes on non-charitable income.
Religious symbols should be allowed in all public institutions.
Ancient scriptures should not be treated as moral authorities today.
Spirituality without organized religion is superior to formal religion.
States should protect religious dress in all public spaces.
Blasphemy should never be criminalized.
Religious leaders should be held to higher moral standards than public officials.
Religious revival would improve social cohesion.
Miracles should be taken seriously as evidence by believers.
Children should not be formally initiated into religions before adolescence.
Secular holidays have replaced religion for many communities.
The separation of church and state is often interpreted too broadly.
Religious charities should be barred from discrimination exemptions.
Public prayer has no place at government events.
Religion does more good than harm in the modern world.
Religious institutions should disclose their finances publicly.
Organized religion provides community in a way secular life struggles to replace.
Religious texts should be reinterpreted rather than discarded when values change.
Clergy should be trained in mental health first response.
States should offer the same public accommodations to minority faiths as majority faiths.
Religious freedom includes the right to refuse participation in sacred rituals.
Most religious conflict is driven more by politics than doctrine.
Pilgrimage remains valuable in the digital age.
Public schools should teach comparative religion.
Belief without evidence can still be rational.
Religious conversion efforts toward vulnerable populations are unethical.
Sacred art and architecture matter even in secular societies.
Confession and repentance are healthier social practices than public apology culture.
Religious identity is becoming more cultural than theological.
The decline of organized religion has left a moral vacuum.
Governments should mediate more actively when religious communities clash.
A universal basic income would improve society.
Wealth taxes are justified in highly unequal societies.
The minimum wage should be indexed automatically to inflation.
Free trade benefits ordinary workers more than protectionism does.
Student debt should be widely forgiven by governments.
Housing should be treated primarily as a social good rather than an investment.
Central banks should target employment more aggressively than inflation.
Gig workers should be classified as employees.
Inheritance taxes should be substantially higher.
The four-day workweek is economically viable at scale.
Price controls are justified during national emergencies.
Automation should be taxed when it replaces human labor.
Monopolies are the biggest threat to innovation.
Governments should subsidize domestic manufacturing even at higher cost.
Billionaires should not exist.
Capital gains should be taxed at the same rate as labor income.
Economic growth is overvalued as a measure of national success.
Rent control does more harm than good.
Public ownership of utilities is preferable to privatization.
Crypto assets have failed as an economic revolution.
Governments should guarantee a job to anyone willing to work.
Offshoring has weakened the middle class.
Luxury taxes are better than broad consumption taxes.
Antitrust enforcement should break up dominant digital platforms.
A recession is sometimes the necessary cost of restoring economic stability.
Stock buybacks should be heavily restricted.
A society can be prosperous without constant GDP growth.
Land value taxes are better than property taxes.
Governments should cap executive compensation ratios in publicly traded firms.
Inflation hurts social trust more than unemployment does.
The housing market should favor residents over investors.
Worker cooperatives deserve more public support than startups.
Economic inequality is a greater threat than public debt.
Sovereign wealth funds should be more common in advanced economies.
Consumer debt is the hidden engine of modern economies.
Tourism-heavy cities should tax short-term rentals more aggressively.
Shareholder primacy has damaged capitalism.
Apprenticeships deserve as much prestige as university degrees.
Economic policy should prioritize resilience over efficiency.
Luxury housing development does not meaningfully solve affordability.
Advertising adds less economic value than its defenders claim.
Retirement savings should default into public low-fee index funds.
Social media has made society less empathetic.
Public shaming is sometimes a legitimate tool for accountability.
Marriage is becoming less necessary as a social institution.
Schools should enforce uniforms to reduce inequality.
Cancel culture is more myth than reality.
Parents should limit smartphone access until high school.
The decline of local communities is the biggest modern social problem.
Children benefit more from structured activities than free play.
Beauty standards do more harm than good.
Society should take loneliness as seriously as unemployment.
Single-sex schools offer advantages that co-ed schools cannot.
People are too quick to cut off friends and family over politics.
Public spaces should discourage car use in favor of community life.
Meritocracy is largely a myth.
The idea of being 'self-made' is socially harmful.
Traditional gender roles still benefit some families.
Online dating has made relationships worse.
The nuclear family model is overrated.
Adults should be expected to care for aging parents whenever possible.
Fame is a net negative for society.
Anonymity online weakens social trust.
Modern society overvalues productivity.
Remote work is eroding community bonds.
Strict school discipline would improve youth outcomes.
Friendship is undervalued compared with romance.
People should delay having children until they are financially stable.
Mass migration reshapes national identity more than governments admit.
Neighborhood design affects happiness more than income does.
Schools should do more to teach practical adulthood.
Public etiquette has declined in the smartphone era.
People owe more to their local communities than to abstract global causes.
Being constantly reachable has weakened personal boundaries.
Shared national rituals are still socially important.
Adults should normalize having fewer but deeper friendships.
Society is too tolerant of chronic lateness and flakiness.
Children should have more household responsibilities.
Modern parenting is too child-centered.
The idea of following your passion is bad advice for most people.
Privacy is becoming a class privilege.
Urban living creates stronger citizens than suburban living.
Status competition drives more behavior than people admit.
Communities should shame blatant antisocial behavior more openly.
College athletes should be treated as employees.
Instant replay improves sports more than it hurts them.
Transnational salary caps would make global sports more fair.
The Olympics should permanently remove judged events.
Sports betting sponsorships should be banned.
Fighting should be penalized more harshly in hockey.
Athletes should be required to speak to the media after games.
Teams should be relegated for sustained poor performance in major U.S. leagues.
The use of performance-enhancing drugs should be medically supervised rather than banned.

College football players should be allowed unlimited transfer freedom.
Traditional rivalries matter more than championships.
Referees should explain controversial calls live to the crowd.
Professional athletes have a duty to use their platforms politically.
Championship playoffs are better than season-long league tables.
Contact sports should be restricted for children under 14.
Salary transparency should be mandatory in professional sports.
The designated hitter improved baseball.
Tanking should be punished by league sanctions.
International competitions matter more than club competitions.
Sportswashing is impossible to separate from modern global sport.
Analytics has made sports less entertaining.
The Hall of Fame should exclude players linked to serious misconduct off the field.
Boxing should be banned.
The best athlete of all time is more a matter of era than achievement.
Women’s sports should receive equal media coverage mandates.
The transfer market hurts competitive balance more than it helps.
Professional leagues should use promotion and relegation systems.
Retired jerseys are overused in modern sports.
The best teams should not be rewarded with favorable playoff seeding advantages.
Athletes should be suspended for gambling on any sport, not just their own.
National team duty should override club obligations.
Sports leagues should shorten seasons to improve quality.
Trash talk is good for sports.
Data tracking has gone too far in athlete monitoring.
Mixed-gender competition should be expanded in selected sports.

Refs should face postgame press conferences.
Olympic amateurism was always a myth.

Franchise relocation should require league and community approval.
Team loyalty is irrational but valuable.
History education should focus more on failures than triumphs.
The atomic bombings were unjustified.
The Roman Empire's legacy is overstated.
Colonialism shaped the modern world more than the Industrial Revolution did.
Historical figures should be judged primarily by the standards of their own time.
The French Revolution did more harm than good.
The American Civil War was inevitable by 1860.
Great man theory is bad history.
Historical monuments should be removed when they glorify oppression.
The Cold War brought more stability than chaos.
Ancient history deserves more space in modern education.
The Renaissance is romanticized.
History classes should prioritize primary sources over textbooks.
Empires are remembered too kindly.
The fall of the Soviet Union was a net positive for the world.
World War I matters more than World War II for understanding the modern era.
Industrialization improved life despite its early cruelty.
National myths are necessary even when historically inaccurate.

History should be taught thematically rather than chronologically.
The concept of progress distorts historical understanding.
Revolutions usually betray their original ideals.
Historical reenactments trivialize suffering.
Museums should return most contested artifacts.
History is too often written from the viewpoint of states rather than ordinary people.
History is best understood through material conditions rather than ideas.
Historical fiction shapes public memory more than textbooks do.
The British Empire should be remembered primarily as a system of extraction.
National holidays usually tell a simplified version of history.
Counterfactual history is a useful academic tool.
The Industrial Revolution's winners are remembered more than its victims.
Ancient democracies deserve less romantic treatment.
Archives should be opened more quickly after major state decisions.
War accelerates social change more than peace does.
Most civilizations collapse gradually rather than suddenly.
History education should spend more time on everyday life than on rulers.
The postwar era is misunderstood as more stable than it really was.
National borders often reflect historical accidents rather than justice.
Historical memory should be treated as a public responsibility.
Some myths are too deeply embedded in nationhood to be fully corrected.
Decolonizing museums is a historical necessity.
The twentieth century was more transformative than any century before it.
Climate change policy should prioritize adaptation over mitigation.
Nuclear power is essential for a low-carbon future.
Meat consumption should be heavily discouraged through taxation.
Rich countries should pay climate reparations to poorer countries.
Geoengineering research should be accelerated.
Plastic bans are largely symbolic rather than transformative.
Cities should eliminate most private cars from downtown areas.
Environmental activism is justified even when it disrupts daily life.
Conservation should prioritize ecosystems over individual animal welfare.
Fast fashion should face strict environmental penalties.
Carbon offsets do more harm than good.
Economic degrowth is necessary to prevent ecological collapse.
Governments should ban the sale of new gas-powered cars within the next decade.
Rewilding projects should take priority over manicured urban green spaces.
Water should never be privatized.
Climate messaging should focus on sacrifice rather than green consumerism.
Tourism taxes should fund environmental repair in fragile destinations.
Captive breeding is a valid conservation strategy.
Developed countries should ban single-use plastics entirely.
Factory farming is incompatible with environmental sustainability.
Renewable energy infrastructure should override local aesthetic objections.
Environmental education should be mandatory through high school.
Air travel should be significantly more expensive.
Nations should count biodiversity loss alongside GDP.
Individual lifestyle changes matter less than corporate regulation.
Environmental policy should focus first on heavy industry rather than households.
Public transit should be free in major cities.
Suburban sprawl is an environmental failure.
National parks should limit tourism more aggressively.
Climate activists should target pension funds as much as oil companies.
Tree planting is too often used as a climate distraction.
Desalination will become essential for many regions.
Wildlife corridors deserve more funding than zoos.
Cities should mandate reflective and cooling roofs.
Environmental law should grant legal rights to rivers and ecosystems.
Carbon labeling should be required on consumer goods.
Renewables alone cannot solve climate change fast enough.
Recycling has been oversold as a solution.
Affluent consumers should face higher environmental taxes than poorer consumers.
Environmental review processes are often too slow for urgent infrastructure.
The greenest building is usually the one already standing.
Climate migration should be recognized in refugee law.

Award shows no longer reflect artistic excellence.
Fan service weakens storytelling.
Remakes are crowding out original creativity.
Artists should be separated from their personal misconduct.

Critics matter more than audience scores for judging art.

Censorship in entertainment is sometimes justified.

Comedy should be allowed to offend without apology.

Piracy can be ethically justified when media is inaccessible.

Cult classics matter more than box-office hits.

Superhero fatigue is a symptom of broader creative stagnation.

Nostalgia is the dominant force in pop culture.

The distinction between high culture and low culture is mostly posturing.

Cultural criticism is healthier when it is less moralized.

Police should be required to carry personal liability insurance.
Restorative justice is preferable to punitive justice for most crimes.
Juveniles should almost never be tried as adults.
Nonviolent drug offenses should not carry prison sentences.
Mandatory minimum sentencing should end.

Prisons should prioritize rehabilitation over punishment.

Laws should treat cybercrime as seriously as physical theft.
Life without parole is a cruel punishment.

Police unions should have less legal protection.
Voting rights should be automatically restored after prison release.

Defamation law should be strengthened against viral misinformation.

Hate crime laws are necessary.
Juries should be allowed to ask questions during trials.

Police should require a college degree or equivalent training standard.
Drug legalization would reduce organized crime more than harsher enforcement.
Courts should move faster even at the cost of some procedural complexity.
Victim-centered justice can conflict with fair trials.

Community policing works better than militarized policing.

Punishment should aim first at public safety rather than moral condemnation.

Surveillance warrants should face much stricter limits.

A fair justice system must tolerate some guilty people going free.

Aging should be treated as a medical condition to be slowed.

Society glamorizes busyness in a way that damages health.

Personal trainers and coaches are worth it for most beginners.

Smartwatches improve health awareness more than they increase anxiety.
Preventive care deserves more funding than late-stage treatment.

Society should normalize rest more than hustle.
